Why is influence marketing central to the EPR?

sections.title: The key recommendations of UMICC
path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Who we are
paragraphs.text: UMICC is the professional organisation representing influence marketing in France. We bring together 150 agencies, creators, agents and influence technologies around a common goal: promote a responsible, transparent and sustainable sector that serves consumers and brands.

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Who we are
paragraphs.text: France has a special place in Europe: with the law of 9 June 2023, we became the first country to give influence to marketing with a clear legal framework, defining the status of influencers, prohibiting certain practices (such as the promotion of tobacco or aesthetic surgery) and increasing transparency. This law marked a turning point, positioning France as a regulatory laboratory for the whole continent.

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Why is influence marketing central to the EPR?
paragraphs.text: Influence marketing is now a **mature and strategic sector** of the French and European digital economy: more than 180,000 creators in France regularly produce content, of which tens of thousands live at least partially. Our industry already accounts for tens of thousands of jobs and is growing strongly (4 050 % a year).

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Why is influence marketing central to the EPR?
paragraphs.text: This dynamism is accompanied by an increased requirement in terms of ethics, transparency and professionalisation. France, thanks to the 2023 law and the charters of ethics set up by UMICC, has already taken an essential step. The EPR must recognise these advances and ensure that a sector that has already been structured, including through self-regulation, is not levelled down.

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
The key recommendations of UMICC
paragraphs.text: Not equating creators with traditional media: content creators are individual entrepreneurs speaking on social platforms. They are neither broadcasters nor press publishers. Imposing on them the same obligations as traditional media would undermine thousands of small creative activities and create inequality with other European players. We are alerting to the abuses already observed in some countries (Italy, Spain) where creators tend to be assimilated to the media. The tax ruling must protect the specificity of this profession.

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
The key recommendations of UMICC
paragraphs.text: Build on the French experience of transparency and consumer protection: French law already requires clear terms ("advertising", "commercial collaboration") and strictly regulates certain promotions. We recommend that the EPR builds on this tried and tested model, adapting it to the realities of each Member State, rather than starting from a blank page.

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
The key recommendations of UMICC
paragraphs.text: Recognising and encouraging professional self-regulation: UMICC has put in place a number of ethical charters, educational tools and support for creators. There are now also certificates for creators and agencies. This dynamic must be encouraged at European level, and responsibility must be shared beyond creators with advertisers, as well as platforms. Rather than increasing heavy legal constraints, the EPR should support and enhance co-regulation mechanisms led by professional organisations.

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
The key recommendations of UMICC
paragraphs.text: Avoiding over-regulation that would hamper innovation: the French sector is already one of the most circumscribed in Europe. Any new European regulation must take account of this acquis and ensure that no disproportionate additional burdens are imposed on creators and SMEs, otherwise innovation will be curtailed and the European market less competitive vis-à-vis US and Asian players.

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
The key recommendations of UMICC
paragraphs.text: Ensuring a fair relationship with platforms: the dependence of creators and brands on platforms remains a major topic: access to audience and performance data, opaque algorithms, unstable monetisation, unilateral suspension decisions. The EPR must establish principles of transparency and fairness, so that European creators can develop their business in a reliable and predictable environment.

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
The key recommendations of UMICC
paragraphs.text: Influence is a sector that creates value, jobs and cultural innovation. France, ahead of its 2023 law and the mobilisation of UMICC, shows that economic development and consumer protection can be combined with an ethical approach.

section_path: Contribution of UMICC (Union des Métiers de l'Influence et des Créateurs de Content) to the public consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
The key recommendations of UMICC
paragraphs.text: We call on the European Commission to recognise this specificity and ensure that the EPR does not transform creators into media, but accompanies them as new players in the digital economy. UMICC stands ready to share its experience, directly or through EIMA, and to contribute to the construction of a European framework that is both protective, fair and growth-friendly.
